```{r}
library("corrplot")
```
Load data:
```{r}
mi_features = readRDS("data/mi_features.rds")
mi_variables = readRDS("data/mi_variables.rds")
```

Identify numerical columns/features: 
```{r}
num_vars = mi_variables[mi_variables$type %in% c("Continuous", "Integer", "Binary"), ]

num_features = mi_features[intersect(names(mi_features),num_vars$name)] #filtering numeric features


```


Different features have different units therefore we need to scale the covariance matrix:
```{r}
num_features <- as.data.frame(
  sapply(num_features, function(x) as.numeric(as.character(x)))
)

pca <- prcomp(num_features, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

summary(pca)
```

The first components do not 'explain' much of the variance.
Which indicate our data is uncorrelated between features, as shown by th correlation plot:
```{r}
R = cor(num_features)
corrplot(R, method = "color", tl.pos = "n")
```
